title: Scale WorkOffer scoring for multiple pawns
summary: Scale WorkOffer lookup, scoring and assignment to multiple pawns with deterministic tie-breaking, bounded visits and ReasonTrace evidence.
acceptance: 20 actors query indexed WorkOffer buckets within explicit candidate and selected-offer caps
Selection ordering is deterministic and stable across equivalent scores
ReasonTrace records candidate totals visited scored cap hits rejection classes selected offer and score for test/debug consumption
No pawn scans all entities all map cells or all work offers
Content scope remains fixture-level and does not add broad work catalogs

history.details: Created by WM-0032 M2 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Make multi-pawn work selection bounded, deterministic and explainable.
benchmarkImpact: Adds 20-pawn WorkOffer metrics for visited/scored counts, cap hits, deterministic selection checksums and trace capacity.
reviewRouting: Owner completes to reviewer; qa-performance must be able to reproduce benchmark artifacts during review.
rollbackModel: Revert task branch and M2 benchmark additions while preserving M1 WorkOffer behavior.
ownerGate: State candidate cap values and complexity in docs
Prove traces are bounded and shared with tests/debug
Run every required check before taskctl complete

sparkEligibility.reason: Multi-pawn deterministic scoring and performance-sensitive selection are not suitable for Spark.
